recent_posts.embed_title: Sync Options - Argo CD - Declarative GitOps CD for Kubernetes

recent_posts.text:
"Doesn't mutating admission violates GitOps principles?"

Kyverno & Mutation is all about contextual reality. While GitOps helps with the desired baseline, we still need to inject dynamic, env-specific logic.

But how to prevent conflicts between the GitOps world and the post-mutation one?

I've been advocating at @payfiteng.bsky.social for us to move our controls from the tooling (OpenTofu lint rules, Helm meta-charts, etc.) to the platform itself (Kubernetes Admission Controllers, AWS Config / SCPs, whatever). Hope to get more insights into how to approach that here!

The great thing with the format of the co-located events for a few years not at #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon is : you got to move between the events! Now to the #KyvernoCon to listen to Rodrigo Fior Kuntzer from Miro about a topic that I love: policies to shift platform logic to the control plane!

AI Runway is aimed at platform teams and AI users who want a Kubernetes-native way to deploy and operate inference workloads across multiple serving frameworks, with a simpler UX and tighter integration with the Kubernetes ecosystem.
Maintainer for AI Runway here 👋. Slurm on Kubernetes is useful if you want HPC-style job scheduling, but it does not provide a common kubernetes inference API or a model-centric control plane.

We run both. Terraform for the foundation. Crossplane for everything devs touch.

Full comparison with architecture diagrams and migration patterns:
podostack.com/p/crossplan... 🛠️

Terraform won't disappear. It's great for bootstrapping - VPCs, accounts, the cluster itself.

But for Day 2 ops? For self-service? For drift protection? That's a control plane problem. Crossplane is a control plane.

Self-service is where it gets real.

Terraform: devs open a PR to the infra repo. SRE reviews. Merges. CI runs apply. Days.

Crossplane: dev runs kubectl apply claim.yaml. Gets a database in minutes. RBAC controls who can claim what.

State management tells the whole story.

Terraform: .tfstate file. Store it in S3, lock it with DynamoDB, pray nobody runs apply concurrently.

Crossplane: state lives in etcd. Same place as your pods. kubectl get it anytime. No lock files.

Terraform: you run plan, review the diff, hit apply. Done. If someone changes infra outside Terraform, you don't know until the next plan.

Crossplane: a control loop runs every 60s. Drift detected, drift corrected. No human in the loop.

terraform apply is a CI job you run and pray.

Crossplane is a controller that never stops reconciling. Someone changes your RDS instance manually? It fixes it in 42 seconds.

podostack.com/p/crossplan...

recent_posts.text:
🔍 Malicious Trivy versions (0.69.4–0.69.6) silently stole SSH keys, cloud creds, CI/CD secrets & more.

Attackers used this access to spread a worm and even deploy destructive Kubernetes payloads.

One compromised tool → full environment exposure.

#Infosec #Kubernetes #Docker #ThreatIntel

🚨 Attackers exploited the Trivy supply chain to spread an infostealer, leading to credential theft and Kubernetes attacks.

If you use Trivy, this may impact your environment.

Read the full breakdown:
basefortify.eu/posts/2026/0...

#CyberSecurity #SupplyChainAttack #DevSecOps #CloudSecurity

記事の要約: AWS（Amazon Web Services）は創立20周年を迎え、240以上のクラウドサービスを提供し続けています。毎年何百万もの顧客に対して新機能をリリースし、ブログには4,700件以上の記事が公開されています。創立当初からのイノベーションのペースは変わらず、特に顧客の重要な課題に対処するソリューションの再考が強調されています。過去10年間の中で、特に注目すべきAWSのローンチやブログも紹介され、コンテナのデプロイやKubernetesサービスの導入など、技術の進化が語られています。

What do  you think influences the growth of the product you are building more…

1) feedback of your users 
2) price
3).showing your face in a social media post 
4) missing out #KubeCon

tell me what you think and then head over to nexus-share.com and create your account 🙃🫶

#BuildInPublic
